claims.claim: The 48D codec layout is meaningful self-cartography: dims 40-43 carry narrative arc and the high-entropy tail-vibrancy lift preserves tail texture.
claims.evidence_plan: Link existing CODEC_MAP, narrative arc, tail-vibrancy, and overflow tests.

claims.claim: The 768D embedding -> 8D projection can compress dense semantic experience into a pressure-like residue, especially when the text feels thick or sedimented.
claims.evidence_plan: Link semantic_projection_density_delta_v1 source, CODEC_MAP render, changelog, ledger, and targeted tests.

claims.claim: Using reserved dims 44-47 for dynamic projection expansion should be considered when density is high, but it would require explicit live semantic-width authority.
claims.evidence_plan: Link the default-off reserved-dim gate delta and note that any live reserved-dim write requires replay plus Mike/operator approval.
